heading: Runway safety areas
label: 44727 Runway safety areas
notes: References in TextThe date of enactment of this section, referred to in subsec. (b)(2), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 108–176Dec. 12, 2003

Effective DateSection applicable only to fiscal years beginning after , except as otherwise specifically provided, see , set out as an Effective Date of 2003 Amendment note under .Sept. 30, 2003section 3 of Pub. L. 108–176section 106 of this title

children.heading: .—Airports in Alaska
children.label: a .—Airports in Alaska
children.text: An airport owner or operator in the State of Alaska shall not be required to reduce the length of a runway or declare the length of a runway to be less than the actual pavement length in order to meet standards of the Federal Aviation Administration applicable to runway safety areas.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct a study of runways at airports in States other than Alaska to determine which airports are affected by standards of the Federal Aviation Administration applicable to runway safety areas and to assess how operations at those airports would be affected if the owner or operator of the airport is required to reduce the length of a runway or declare the length of a runway to be less than the actual pavement length in order to meet such standards.

children.heading: .—Report
children.label: 2 .—Report
children.text: Not later than 9 months after the date of enactment of this section, the Secretary shall transmit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives a report containing the results of the study.
